commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer handling, a concrete test repair, and new dependency/security acceptance are needed before this PR should merge.; Needs attention: The diff adds a new root image-processing package and package-manager build/release-age exceptions, so maintainer supply-chain acceptance is still needed.; Review finding: [P2] Port the security test to the Rastermill seam — src/media/image-ops.ts:1-8 (sha=f26b73908540114cf644ea2738f0ea46301051f4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces OpenClaw image processing with a `rastermill@0.1.0` adapter, updates root dependency and package-manager policy files, and adds one Rastermill adapter test.

PR surface: Source -1220, Tests +31, Config +2, Other +19. Total -1168 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: the PR removes the modules mocked by `src/media/image-ops.security.test.ts`, while that test still expects a mocked plugin error from `resizeToJpeg`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer handling, a concrete test repair, and new dependency/security acceptance are needed before this PR should merge.

target.title: refactor: use Rastermill for image processing
target.body:
## Summary

- Move OpenClaw image processing to the published npm package `rastermill@0.1.0`.
- Delete the in-repo image processor implementation from `src/media/image-ops.ts`; OpenClaw now uses a small Rastermill adapter while keeping trusted native command resolution.
- Preserve input/output pixel limits, `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_BACKEND`, Windows/native backend aliases, best-effort EXIF normalization, and central availability-error classification.

## Verification

- Rastermill npm: `npm view rastermill@0.1.0 name version dist.tarball dist.integrity dist.unpackedSize dependencies --json`
- Rastermill package: `pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm build && npm pack --dry-run`
- Rastermill publish: `npm publish --access public` published `rastermill@0.1.0`
- OpenClaw focused tests: `pnpm test src/media/image-ops.rastermill-adapter.test.ts src/media/image-ops.input-guard.test.ts src/media/image-ops.helpers.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts test/package-manager-config.test.ts test/scripts/check-dependency-pins.test.ts test/scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts`
- OpenClaw TUI PTY: `timeout 120s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`
- OpenClaw build: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm build`
- Auto review: `autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer owns its image-processing Photon implementation directly; core media image operations delegate to the public npm package `rastermill@0.1.0` while preserving safety and backend behavior.
Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node/pnpm repo defaults, real `rastermill@0.1.0` dependency fetched from npm.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install --lockfile-only`; `node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs`; focused `pnpm test ...`; `timeout 120s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`; `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm build`; `autoreview --mode local`; npm registry verification for `rastermill@0.1.0`.
Evidence after fix: npm reports `rastermill@0.1.0` at `https://registry.npmjs.org/rastermill/-/rastermill-0.1.0.tgz` with integrity `sha512-ovbl5jVRMc0LnzktQMU13Nz5c9iPbHi9PoT4hG8qiyOGVM6a20I6N2TtaFJv40B38LLF29luN/E8NmNIXk7lCg==`; focused OpenClaw tests passed 5 Vitest shards; TUI PTY passed 11 tests; build completed; autoreview was clean.
Observed result after fix: image metadata/resizing/PNG optimization/agent attachment paths remain green, native fallback commands still route through OpenClaw trusted resolution, shrinkwraps resolve the public npm package, and the TUI fixture can resolve Rastermill through tsx/Node loader probing.
What was not tested: Windows `windows-native` PowerShell execution was preserved in Rastermill and typechecked, but not live-run on a Windows host in this local pass. `pnpm check:changed` currently reaches unrelated latest-main type errors in `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts` and `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`; those files are not changed by this PR.
target.author: steipete
